[Yeda](/wiki/Yeda_Peak "Yeda Peak")

([French](/wiki/French_language "French language"): *Chaîne des Cassiars*) are the most northerly group of the [Northern Interior Mountains](/wiki/Interior_Mountains "Interior Mountains") in the [Canadian province](/wiki/Canadian_province "Canadian province") of [British Columbia](/wiki/British_Columbia "British Columbia") and also extend slightly into the southernmost [Yukon Territory](/wiki/Yukon "Yukon"). They lie north and west of the [Omineca Mountains](/wiki/Omineca_Mountains "Omineca Mountains"), west of the northernmost Rockies and the [Rocky Mountain Trench](/wiki/Rocky_Mountain_Trench "Rocky Mountain Trench"), north of the [Hazelton Mountains](/wiki/Hazelton_Mountains "Hazelton Mountains") and east of the [Boundary Ranges](/wiki/Boundary_Ranges "Boundary Ranges"). They form a section of the [Continental Divide](/wiki/Continental_Divide_of_the_Americas "Continental Divide of the Americas"), that, in this region, separates water drainage between the Arctic and Pacific Oceans. Physiographically, they are a section of the larger Yukon-Tanana Uplands province, which in turn are part of the larger [Intermontane Plateaus](/wiki/Intermontane_Plateaus "Intermontane Plateaus") physiographic division.

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Mountain range in British Columbia and Yukon Territory, Canada

The **Cassiar Mountains** ([French](/wiki/French_language "French language"): *Chaîne des Cassiars*) are the most northerly group of the [Northern Interior Mountains](/wiki/Interior_Mountains "Interior Mountains") in the [Canadian province](/wiki/Canadian_province "Canadian province") of [British Columbia](/wiki/British_Columbia "British Columbia") and also extend slightly into the southernmost [Yukon Territory](/wiki/Yukon "Yukon"). They lie north and west of the [Omineca Mountains](/wiki/Omineca_Mountains "Omineca Mountains"), west of the northernmost Rockies and the [Rocky Mountain Trench](/wiki/Rocky_Mountain_Trench "Rocky Mountain Trench"), north of the [Hazelton Mountains](/wiki/Hazelton_Mountains "Hazelton Mountains") and east of the [Boundary Ranges](/wiki/Boundary_Ranges "Boundary Ranges"). They form a section of the [Continental Divide](/wiki/Continental_Divide_of_the_Americas "Continental Divide of the Americas"), that, in this region, separates water drainage between the Arctic and Pacific Oceans. Physiographically, they are a section of the larger Yukon-Tanana Uplands province, which in turn are part of the larger [Intermontane Plateaus](/wiki/Intermontane_Plateaus "Intermontane Plateaus") physiographic division.

In the western Cassiar Mountains lie the remnants of a prehistoric [shield volcano](/wiki/Shield_volcano "Shield volcano") called the [Maitland Volcano](/wiki/Maitland_Volcano "Maitland Volcano") which formed between 5 and 4 million years ago during the [Pliocene](/wiki/Pliocene "Pliocene") period.

The highest mountain in the Cassiar Mountains is [Thudaka Peak](/wiki/Thudaka_Peak "Thudaka Peak"), at 2,748 m (9,016 ft).[[1]](#cite_note-1)
